utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: patient is julia jones , date of birth 5/16/1996 . she is a new breast reduction consult . hello , how are you doing today ? i'm dr. gonzalez . it's nice to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to meet you , ma'am . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing good , thank you . so you're here to discuss a breast reduction , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . that's right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . perfect . let me just get logged in , here . have you met with anyone before about a breast reduction ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i have n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how long have you been considering this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's probably been about six or seven years . but i mean , it's always been in the back of my mind .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , i perform a lot of breast reduction surgeries , pretty much every week , so it is a very common procedure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that's reassuring .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so what i wan na do first is just get a little bit of history about the sort of symptoms that you're having . and then i'll do a quick exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then we'll talk more about the surgery and what it entails . and , um , at this point , who is your insurance provider ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have blue cross blue shield . well , actually , my husband just accepted a new job , and i will be on his plan , uh , which i believe is not going to be blue cross , but it may be changing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . what we typically do when we do a breast reduction consult , i'll document your symptoms , symptoms , we'll take some pictures , then , um , they get sent to the insurance company . and what they'll do is decide whether it's approved , or medically necessary , or not . but we wan na make sure we send it to the right insurance . do you know when you'll be on the new plan ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: most likely july .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so we may just wait until july to submit for approval at that time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so regarding your symptoms , do you experience back pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i've been going to the chiropractor for a few years . i do have a lotta pain here and here . but i do n't know if it's all from my breasts or not . it gets painful and builds up . so i think i could attribute it to my breasts , when i'm working at my desk all day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and in your neck and your upper back . and how long has this been going on ? would you say five years , 10 years , or even since puberty ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would say since i was in high school .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do you have the bra strap indentations in your shoulders ? um , i saw that you're wearing about a g cup .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . i do get indentations , but i do n't really wear a regular bra that often . i mostly were a 34 e sports bra because it's more comfortable . and the others are expensive in my size .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i have heard that as well . and , um , have you had any children , or are you planning to have any children in the future ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do not have any kids now . but we might plan to in the future .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and at this time , are you experiencing any numbness or tingling in the s- in the hands or any nipple pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes , yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and rashes , do you get any rashes underneath your breasts ? and , and it could be from sweating .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , but they are always sweaty .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then do you feel like your activities are limited because of the size ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: there's certain things i do when i have to hold them , like working out and any jumping or running . and it hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and it hurts , okay . and , and you saw a chiropractor . but what about physical therapy for your back pain ? or do you take any pain medicine for it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: y- no , but i do take a migraine medication , propranolol .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's okay . so let's see . i know that you have a history of breast cancer in your family . have you had any mammograms ? and how old was your mother when she had it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mom was 40 . and they recommended me starting at age 30 for mammogram . i'm still ... i mean , i'm 25 . so i'm still a little bit young .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and otherwise , it seems like you're really healthy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , for the most part . i lost about 10 pounds over the past few months , and i still would like to lose about 20 more pounds .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: congratulations . that's great . and then , um-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . so then ... you're welcome . and so do you use any tobacco , drugs , or alcohol ? and then , um , you said that you were going to school . what are you going to school for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i drink a little wine on occasion , and for school , i'm currently studying psychology .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that's great . all right . so what we'll do is we'll go ahead and take a look . i'm gon na take a couple of measurements . and we'll kinda talk about the surgery afterwards . um , so go ahead and stand up for me , julia . okay . so , looking at the measurements , it looks like one breast is a little lower than the other .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , thank you so much . it looks like i have all the measurements that we need . you can go ahead and cover up now .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i think you're a great candidate for a breast reduction . when we talk about a breast reduction , what happens is that we remove the tissue out of the breast .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we would then lift them and elevate the nipple position in order to help with your back pain , neck pain , and because i'm a plastic surgeon , of course i want them to look nice as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so typically , when you do the breast reduction , we make an incision around the nipple straight down and then underneath . and it kinda looks like , um , an anchor below . then straight down , and underneath , and through that incision , we're able to f- to lift the breast . and we'll take off any extra fat and breash- breast tissue . so that way it becomes smaller in size . and i noticed that you would like to be a b cup . so i do tell every patient we can make you as small as your blood supply allows . and what that means is we must move the nipple without cutting off any of its blood supply during the surgery .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . well , thank you for explaining that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . and in your case , you'll probably be a small c. um , a b cup might be a little bit small , but we'll see once i'm doing the , the procedure for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and typically the surgery takes about three hours . you will have drains , one in each side . and that helps prevent fluid from building up in the breast . and that stays in for about a week . and then we'll remove them in the clinic . and you'll have a clear plastic tape over your incision that should help with the scars . and , um , we've seen that patients who have lighter skin , the scars will tend to be red at first . and then it takes about a year for a scar to mature in line .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . well , i'm not too worried about the scars .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i do like to keep patients overnight . it's just going to be for one night in the hospital . and we just wan na make sure your pain is controlled , make sure you're not nauseated , all of that stuff . some patients wan na go home that same day . but you know that some people get nauseated , and the last thing that i want is for you to be vomiting at home alone without the , the support here at the facility .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , yeah , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then about 30 % of patients say that they can not breastfeed after a breast reduction , and the reason is the breast is made up of fat . and it's also made up of a gland . and the gland is what produces the milk .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and so in order to reduce the size of the breast , we have to take out both the fat and the glands . so it just depends on how much is left , whether you can breastfeed in the future . and then also , the breast will change over time . if you , um , gain weight , they will get larger , things like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and if you have children , they will change as well . they will enlarge , especially if you can breastfeed . but they'll go back down and will appear , um , a little bit deflated .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i do n't know if i'm interested in breastfeeding .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , the hormones of pregnancy , though , will change the breasts , so that's just something to be aware of , and i tell all younger patients that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , yeah . absolutely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then typically nipple sensation is fine after a breast reduction , but there is a slight chance that you'll have an alteration in the nipple sensation or not have any sensation in the nipple . generally the nipple is just fine unless it is a massive reduction . and it's quite normal after surgery to be a little bit bruised , and then it just takes some time for that to go away .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but other than that , you know , most patients are very happy after the breast reduction because the symptoms of the back pain , the neck pain , you can feel relief almost immediately . so do you have any questions , julia , about the process or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , i do think you're a good candidate for it . and i think you'll benefit from it as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . i look forward to a relief .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i think you just have to do it when you are ready , when you know that you feel like it's a good time , because it is a commitment , and you will have some activity restrictions for about six weeks after surgery , uh , no heavy lifting . and i do say no driving for two to three weeks . and the drains stay in , like i said , for about a week .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . well , i am ready as soon as my insurance is switched over .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , i think from this point , if you want to , we can take photos today , while you're here . i have all the documentation now in the note about your symptoms , and that's what the insurance company is going to look for . so thank you for answering those questions . so once we have the new provider , we'll go ahead and get that submitted . and then , when they approve it , um , what the office will do , is they'll work with you on a surgery date , and then we'll meet again right before the surgery to answer any last minute questions and go over the instructions in more detail , things like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . well , i will notify the office as soon as i have the insurance information .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that sounds great . and then we can get it all in process . and it was so nice to meet you . and catherine will be right in to get your photos . julia jones is a 25 year old female with symptomatic macromastia , presenting for evaluation of breast reduction . the patient has attempted nonsurgical treatments , but the minimal relief , and is an ideal candidate for bilateral reduction mammoplasty . her minimum required resection , based on a bsa of 2.65 , with the schnur scale is 1,792 grams , which should be attainable . given her extremely large breasts and the measurements above , there is potential for using a nipple graft .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: physical exam findings of breast , inspection reveals asymmetrical breasts with severe ptosis bilaterally . there is no nipple retraction or discharge bilaterally . no breast tenderness , masses , or axillary lymphadenopathy is palpable .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 25-year-old female presenting for a breast reduction consultation.
Breast reduction
The doctor asks if the patient has previously consulted with anyone regarding a breast reduction.
The doctor asks how long the patient has been considering a breast reduction.
The patient has been considering the procedure for six to seven years.
The doctor asks for the patient's current insurance provider.
The patient currently has Blue Cross Blue Shield but expects to switch to her spouse's insurance plan.
The doctor asks when the patient will be on the new insurance plan.
The patient expects to be on the new insurance plan in July.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences back pain.
The patient reports chronic upper back and neck pain, managed by a chiropractor for a few years, which she attributes to her breast size.
The doctor asks about the duration of the patient's back and neck pain.
The patient has experienced back and neck pain since high school.
The doctor asks if the patient has bra strap indentations and notes her current G cup size.
The patient reports occasional bra strap indentations and primarily wears a 34E sports bra for comfort.
The doctor asks if the patient has children or plans to have children in the future.
The patient has no children but may plan to have them in the future.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences numbness or tingling in the hands or nipple pain.
The patient reports occasional numbness, tingling, and nipple pain.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences rashes underneath the breasts.
The patient denies rashes but reports persistent sweating under the breasts.
The doctor asks if the patient's physical activities are limited by breast size.
The patient reports that physical activities like jumping or running are painful and limited by breast size.
The doctor asks if the patient has undergone physical therapy or takes pain medication for her back pain.
The patient has not undergone physical therapy and does not take pain medication for her back, but takes propranolol for migraines.
The doctor asks about the patient's family history of breast cancer and mammogram history.
The patient's mother had breast cancer at age 40, and the patient is advised to start mammograms at age 30.
The patient has lost 10 pounds recently and desires to lose 20 more.
The doctor asks about the patient's use of tobacco, drugs, alcohol, and her current field of study.
The patient drinks wine socially and is studying psychology.
The doctor performs a physical exam and notes breast asymmetry.
The doctor confirms the patient is a candidate for breast reduction surgery.
The doctor explains the surgical procedure, including incisions and the goal of reducing breast size.
The doctor estimates the patient will likely be a small C cup post-surgery.
The doctor explains the surgery duration, use of drains, and scar healing process.
The doctor recommends an overnight hospital stay for pain management and monitoring.
The doctor explains the potential impact of breast reduction on future breastfeeding.
The doctor discusses the potential for altered nipple sensation post-surgery.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions about the process.
The doctor outlines post-operative activity restrictions, including no heavy lifting for six weeks and no driving for two to three weeks.
The doctor plans to take photos today, submit for insurance approval once the new plan is active, and schedule a pre-operative visit.
The patient will notify the office once she has her new insurance information.
